parameters.content:
# How to get this workflow working

The webhook must be an HTTPS url

## 1. Stop the HTTP n8n if running

```
docker ps stop n8n
```

## 2. Get an HTTPS url with `ngrok`
See my own account: https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/setup/linux 

```
ngrok http 5678
```

## 3. Create the HTTPS n8n, delete the existing one first AND replace the webhook url {LOCALTUNNEL_URL} with the localtunnel one just created.

```
docker stop n8n-telegram-with-localtunnel-webhook
docker rm n8n-telegram-with-localtunnel-webhook
docker run -d \
  --name n8n-telegram-with-localtunnel-webhook \
  -p 5678:5678 \
  -v /home/meme/MyProjects/n8n-server-2/n8n-data:/home/node/.n8n \
  -e N8N_PORT=5678 \
  -e WEBHOOK_URL={NGROK_URL} \
  -e N8N_BASIC_AUTH_ACTIVE=false \
  n8nio/n8n:latest
```

## Execution within the docker container

`wget` already part of the docker images
`ffmpeg` installed manually for now with `apk update && apk add ffmpeg`
`curl` installed manually for now with `apk update && apk add curl`

### TODO
* Install `ffmpeg` and `curl` as part of container setup. say in a docker-compose.yml file
